que: What is Provider commonly used for in Flutter?
options: Sharing and managing state in the widget tree

que: Which class is often used with Provider for simple shared state?
Which widget is commonly used to provide a ChangeNotifier to descendants?
What is context.watch<T>() mainly used for?
options: Reading a value and rebuilding when it changes
Creating a new provider every frame

que: What is context.read<T>() mainly used for?
options: Reading a value without listening for rebuilds

que: Which practice should you avoid when using Provider?
options: Keeping logic in the provider model

que: What is a good reason to use Provider instead of only local setState()?
options: To share state across multiple widgets more cleanly
To remove the need for widgets
To force all widgets to rebuild
